title: Screens harm development vs technology enables learning
dek: What adult-guided task, for which learner, replaces what prior activity, and with what measured learning gain?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether digital mediation is inherently developmentally costly or a scalable way to increase practice, feedback, and access.

continuum.explanation: Technology effects depend on what activity the tool replaces and whether adult-guided instructional design is present.

whatEvidenceSuggests: The evidence is mixed: device access and some laptop programs can help, but technology alone is not an instructional model; K-12 generative AI evidence is still early.
betterQuestion: What adult-guided task, for which learner, replaces what prior activity, and with what measured learning gain?
commonMisreadings: active, teacher-mediated technology and AI is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Passive/replacement screen time is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Whole language / balanced literacy vs phonics / Science of Reading
dek: How do we guarantee code mastery without narrowing reading to decoding drills?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether reading is learned naturally through rich literacy exposure or requires systematic instruction in the alphabetic code.

continuum.rightPole: explicit code-based instruction plus language comprehension
continuum.explanation: Strongly favors explicit code instruction, while keeping vocabulary, fluency, comprehension, and background knowledge in the model.

whatEvidenceSuggests: The evidence strongly supports explicit foundational skills, but reading comprehension also requires vocabulary, background knowledge, fluency, and text experience.
betterQuestion: How do we guarantee code mastery without narrowing reading to decoding drills?
commonMisreadings: explicit code-based instruction plus language comprehension is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Meaning-first immersion and cueing is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Knowledge-rich curriculum vs generic skills
dek: What content sequence gives students the background knowledge needed to transfer skills?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether comprehension and reasoning are mostly general skills or depend on accumulated content knowledge.

continuum.rightPole: teach transferable strategies like critical thinking
continuum.explanation: Comprehension and reasoning depend heavily on domain knowledge; generic strategies alone are too thin.

whatEvidenceSuggests: Knowledge-rich approaches are gaining causal support; generic skills are easier to name than to teach without content.
betterQuestion: What content sequence gives students the background knowledge needed to transfer skills?
commonMisreadings: teach transferable strategies like critical thinking is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Build domain knowledge and vocabulary is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Direct instruction vs inquiry/project-based learning
dek: For which content, prior knowledge level, and assessment type does inquiry beat explicit teaching?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether teachers should first explain and scaffold or let students construct understanding through inquiry.

continuum.explanation: Novices usually need explicit modeling and practice; inquiry works best when content-rich and scaffolded.

whatEvidenceSuggests: Novices often need explicit instruction; inquiry and PBL can work when content-rich and heavily scaffolded.
betterQuestion: For which content, prior knowledge level, and assessment type does inquiry beat explicit teaching?
commonMisreadings: student exploration and authentic projects is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Explicit modeling and practice is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Standardization/accountability vs professional autonomy
dek: Which outcomes should be common and which decisions should remain local?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether coherent external pressure improves equity or narrows learning and undermines professionalism.

continuum.explanation: Transparent standards and measurement help, but high-stakes systems narrow behavior and invite gaming.

whatEvidenceSuggests: Accountability can focus attention and raise some math outcomes, but high-stakes systems create threshold gaming and curriculum narrowing.
betterQuestion: Which outcomes should be common and which decisions should remain local?
commonMisreadings: local discretion and teacher judgment is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Common standards, tests, and consequences is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Choice/markets vs common public system
dek: What school model and accountability system is being chosen, not just what governance label?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether family choice and school autonomy create improvement or undermine shared capacity and equity.

continuum.explanation: Effects depend on school quality, accountability rules, admissions, transportation, and outcome definition.

whatEvidenceSuggests: Effects are highly heterogeneous: some urban charters are strong, while voucher effects vary sharply by state and participating schools.
betterQuestion: What school model and accountability system is being chosen, not just what governance label?
commonMisreadings: neighborhood public schools is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Charters/vouchers/magnets is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Central curriculum / HQIM vs teacher-created materials
dek: What adaptations preserve the high-leverage content and task quality of the curriculum?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether coherence and quality control outweigh teacher autonomy and adaptation.

continuum.leftPole: Adopt vetted materials and aligned PD
continuum.rightPole: let teachers design locally responsive curricula
continuum.explanation: Materials quality matters, but procurement without use, training, and buy-in has weak effects.

whatEvidenceSuggests: Materials quality matters, but procurement without use, training, and buy-in has weak effects.
betterQuestion: What adaptations preserve the high-leverage content and task quality of the curriculum?
commonMisreadings: let teachers design locally responsive curricula is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Adopt vetted materials and aligned PD is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Remediation/readiness vs grade-level acceleration
dek: What support lets students succeed in grade-level content without tracking them into low expectations?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether students below grade level need prerequisite repair or exposure to rigorous current work.

continuum.leftPole: Backfill missing skills before grade-level work
continuum.rightPole: provide grade-level access with just-in-time support
continuum.explanation: Backfilling forever can trap students; access to grade-level work needs tutoring and scaffolding.

whatEvidenceSuggests: Access-only reforms can backfire without support; tutoring and scaffolding can make acceleration credible.
betterQuestion: What support lets students succeed in grade-level content without tracking them into low expectations?
commonMisreadings: provide grade-level access with just-in-time support is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Backfill missing skills before grade-level work is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Discipline/order vs restorative/relational climate
dek: How do we reduce exclusion while preserving safety and time-on-task?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether safety and instructional time require exclusionary consequences or can be built through relationships.

continuum.explanation: Reducing exclusion matters, but safety and instructional time cannot be waved away.

whatEvidenceSuggests: PBIS and restorative approaches can improve climate and reduce suspensions, but academic effects depend on implementation and grade level.
betterQuestion: How do we reduce exclusion while preserving safety and time-on-task?
commonMisreadings: repair, belonging, and reduced exclusion is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Clear consequences and order is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: SEL/whole-child vs academics-first
dek: Which nonacademic supports measurably increase instructional engagement and persistence?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether nonacademic skills are prerequisites for learning or distractions from content.

continuum.leftPole: Teach social-emotional skills and provide supports
continuum.explanation: SEL is strongest when it improves engagement and learning conditions, not when it substitutes for academics.

whatEvidenceSuggests: SEL evidence is positive on average, but the strongest designs treat SEL as a complement to instruction, not a substitute.
betterQuestion: Which nonacademic supports measurably increase instructional engagement and persistence?
commonMisreadings: focus tightly on academic instruction is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Teach social-emotional skills and provide supports is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Teacher incentives/evaluation vs trust/professionalism
dek: Which measures are for development, which for personnel decisions, and how are errors handled?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether teaching quality improves through measurement and consequences or through professional learning and working conditions.

continuum.explanation: Measurement can help, but coaching has more consistent developmental evidence than simple merit pay.

whatEvidenceSuggests: Coaching has more consistent developmental evidence than simple merit pay; evaluation systems can change workforce composition but carry trust costs.
betterQuestion: Which measures are for development, which for personnel decisions, and how are errors handled?
commonMisreadings: autonomy, collaboration, coaching is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Value-added, observations, incentives, consequences is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Human tutoring vs AI tutoring
dek: Can AI raise dosage or teacher capacity without replacing high-value human interaction?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether the human relationship is core to tutoring or an expensive delivery mechanism that AI can approximate.

continuum.explanation: Human high-dosage tutoring has much stronger causal evidence; AI may scale practice and feedback but is not equivalent yet.

whatEvidenceSuggests: High-dosage human tutoring has much stronger K-12 causal evidence; AI is promising but not yet equivalently validated.
betterQuestion: Can AI raise dosage or teacher capacity without replacing high-value human interaction?
commonMisreadings: scalable AI feedback and practice is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Adult relationship and diagnosis is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Early academics vs play-based pre-K
dek: Which adult-child interactions and curricula produce both readiness and later well-being?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether early academic instruction is developmentally inappropriate or necessary for readiness.

continuum.explanation: The evidence favors intentional instruction embedded in development, not worksheets or purely unstructured play.

whatEvidenceSuggests: Pre-K effects depend on quality, curriculum, and counterfactual care; Boston and Tennessee show scaled pre-K can produce very different long-run results.
betterQuestion: Which adult-child interactions and curricula produce both readiness and later well-being?
commonMisreadings: developmental play and social interaction is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Intentional literacy/math instruction is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: College-for-all vs CTE/career pathways
dek: Are students getting portable credentials, academic rigor, and real postsecondary options?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether career pathways track students away from college or make academics more relevant and valuable.

continuum.explanation: Strong career pathways avoid dead-end tracking by preserving rigor, credits, and portable credentials.

whatEvidenceSuggests: High-quality CTE and early college can improve transitions; weak pathways risk low-value credentials or tracking.
betterQuestion: Are students getting portable credentials, academic rigor, and real postsecondary options?
commonMisreadings: career-themed applied learning is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Academic college-prep sequence is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Small schools/personalization vs comprehensive breadth
dek: Which functions need smallness, and which can be shared across a larger system?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether personalization outweighs lost breadth and operational complexity.

continuum.rightPole: large schools with more courses, activities, and economies of scale
continuum.explanation: NYC small schools are a serious positive case, but smallness itself is not the whole mechanism.

whatEvidenceSuggests: NYC small schools show strong high school outcomes when choice and implementation align; not all small-school conversions replicate this.
betterQuestion: Which functions need smallness, and which can be shared across a larger system?
commonMisreadings: large schools with more courses, activities, and economies of scale is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Smaller relationships and coherence is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Data-driven continuous improvement vs big-bang reform
dek: What problem is narrow enough for CI, and when is structural redesign required?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether change should be iterative and contextual or broad and decisive.

continuum.leftPole: Rapid cycles, local testing, network learning
continuum.explanation: Continuous improvement is strong for targeted problems; structural redesign may still be needed for systemic failures.

whatEvidenceSuggests: NSI evidence is promising for targeted outcomes, but impact depends on engagement, disciplined cycles, and intermediary quality.
betterQuestion: What problem is narrow enough for CI, and when is structural redesign required?
commonMisreadings: sweeping top-down redesign is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Rapid cycles, local testing, network learning is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Integration/desegregation vs neighborhood/local control
dek: Which student outcomes count: achievement, opportunity, civic contact, safety, or legitimacy?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether equal opportunity requires mixing students across race/class or strengthening neighborhood schools.

continuum.explanation: Test scores are not the only outcome; civic contact, peer composition, and access to resources also matter.

whatEvidenceSuggests: Integration can affect peers, resources, and expectations, but test-score-only evaluation misses civic and social goals.
betterQuestion: Which student outcomes count: achievement, opportunity, civic contact, safety, or legitimacy?
commonMisreadings: local assignment and community control is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Diverse, intentionally integrated schools is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Tracking/acceleration vs detracking/inclusion
dek: How do schools preserve rigor and access while giving different students different supports?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether grouping protects pace and fit or institutionalizes low expectations.

continuum.rightPole: keep access to rigorous shared courses
continuum.explanation: Detracking without support can fail, while tracking can institutionalize low expectations.

whatEvidenceSuggests: Algebra-for-all evidence warns that detracking without supports can increase failures; acceleration must include scaffolds.
betterQuestion: How do schools preserve rigor and access while giving different students different supports?
commonMisreadings: keep access to rigorous shared courses is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Sort students by readiness is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Bilingual dual-language vs English immersion
dek: What is the time horizon, and are we measuring English-only achievement or biliteracy plus academics?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether home-language development supports cognition and identity or slows English academic progress.

continuum.explanation: The conclusion depends on time horizon and whether biliteracy itself is treated as an outcome.

whatEvidenceSuggests: Evaluation must separate language proficiency, content learning, long-run attainment, and family/community goals.
betterQuestion: What is the time horizon, and are we measuring English-only achievement or biliteracy plus academics?
commonMisreadings: concentrate on rapid English acquisition is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Maintain bilingual development and biliteracy is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: More money/resources vs 'money does not matter'
dek: Which marginal dollars buy the highest-value inputs in this context?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether resource constraints are causal or secondary to how schools use existing resources.

continuum.rightPole: focus on incentives, efficiency, and governance
continuum.explanation: Sustained targeted dollars can improve outcomes, but spending category and implementation are decisive.

whatEvidenceSuggests: Court-reform evidence indicates sustained targeted dollars can improve outcomes, but money still needs coherent use.
betterQuestion: Which marginal dollars buy the highest-value inputs in this context?
commonMisreadings: focus on incentives, efficiency, and governance is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Increase targeted spending and staffing is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.

title: Seat time vs mastery
dek: What external outcomes verify mastery when internal grades are redesigned?
philosophicalDisagreement: Whether learning systems should be organized around exposure or competence.

continuum.leftPole: Credit for time and course completion
continuum.explanation: Mastery is philosophically strong but hard to validate because credits and grades can change meaning.

whatEvidenceSuggests: Mastery models are philosophically compelling but hard to evaluate because grades, credits, and pacing rules change meaning.
betterQuestion: What external outcomes verify mastery when internal grades are redesigned?
commonMisreadings: progression after demonstrated mastery is not a magic phrase; the mechanism and implementation still matter.
Credit for time and course completion is not automatically wrong in every context; outcomes and students differ.
whatWouldChangeOurMind: Comparable causal evidence across settings showing durable gains on achievement, progression, and implementation quality would move this synthesis.
